resources.string:
"
Cricket Rules
Cricket is played between two teams of eleven players each on a field at the centre of which is a 22-yard pitch.
Objective
The batting team scores runs by hitting the ball with the bat and running between the wickets, while the bowling and fielding team tries to dismiss each batter.
Scoring
1-3 Runs: Running between wickets.
4 Runs: Ball hits the ground and then crosses the boundary.
6 Runs: Ball crosses the boundary without hitting the ground.
Dismissals
Common ways to get out: Bowled, Caught, LBW (Leg Before Wicket), Run Out, and Stumped. "

"
Football Rules
Football (Soccer) is a game played by two teams of eleven players using a round ball.
Objective
The object of the game is to score by maneuvering the ball into the opposing goal.
Key Rules
No Hands: Players cannot use their hands or arms (except the goalkeeper).
Match Duration: A standard match consists of two 45-minute halves.
Fouls: Physical contact like tripping or pushing results in a free kick or penalty.
"

"
Basketball Rules
Basketball is played by two teams of five players on a rectangular court.
Objective
The objective is to shoot a ball through a hoop that is 10 feet high.
Scoring
2 Points: Field goal made from inside the three-point line.
3 Points: Field goal made from outside the three-point line.
1 Point: Free throw.
Violations
Common violations include Traveling, Double Dribbling, and personal fouls. "

"
Volleyball Rules
Volleyball is a team sport in which two teams of six players are separated by a net.
Objective
Each team tries to score points by grounding a ball on the opposing team\'s court under organized rules.
Key Rules
Three Hits: A team is allowed three hits to return the ball.
No Double Hit: A player cannot hit the ball twice in succession.
Rotation: Players rotate clockwise after winning a serve.
"

"
Hockey Rules
Field Hockey is played by two teams of eleven players using sticks to maneuver a hard ball.
Objective
To score more goals than the opponent by hitting the ball into the opponent\'s goal.
Key Rules
Flat Side: Players must only use the flat side of their stick.
Feet: Players (except the goalie) cannot use their feet to play the ball.
Scoring: A goal is scored only if hit from within the \"shooting circle\".
"

"
Tennis Rules
Tennis is a racket sport that can be played individually against a single opponent or between two teams of two players each.
Objective
To hit the ball over the net and into the opponent\'s court in a way that they cannot return it.
Scoring System
Points: Love (0), 15, 30, 40, Game.
Deuce: A tie at 40-40, requiring a two-point lead to win.
Sets: A match is usually best of 3 or 5 sets.
"

"
Badminton Rules
Badminton is a racket sport played using rackets to hit a shuttlecock across a net.
Objective
To hit the shuttlecock over the net and land it within the opponent\'s court boundaries.
Key Rules
Service: The serve must be hit underarm and below the waist.
Let: A rally is replayed if an unexpected disturbance occurs.
Winning: Matches are usually best of three games to 21 points.
"
